commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: inject agents into AbstractSearchOrchestrator to resolve NPE

keywordGeneratorAgent, snippetExtensionAgent, summaryContextAgent,
searchResultsAssessmentAgent and contextOrchestrator were declared as
protected fields in AbstractSearchOrchestrator but never populated,
because the orchestrators are instantiated via 'new' inside @Provides
methods — Dagger does not inject fields of objects it doesn't manage.

Changes:
- AbstractSearchOrchestrator: add initAgents() method to wire all agent deps
- AIAgentsModule: accept agent params in @Provides for ConfluenceSearch-
  Orchestrator and TrackerSearchOrchestrator, call initAgents() after 'new'
- Expert: @Inject KeywordGeneratorAgent/SnippetExtensionAgent/
  SummaryContextAgent/SearchResultsAssessmentAgent; call initAgents()
  on CodebaseSearchOrchestrator created inline in extendContextWithCode()

Also fix pre-existing test failures from merged upstream commit:
- TestCasesGeneratorParams: add missing customTestCasesTracker field
  (CustomTestCasesTrackerParamsTest expected it but it was never added)
- TestCasesGeneratorParamsTest: add null arg for new field to @AllArgsConstructor call
- TestCasesGeneratorParallelTest: remove setField for non-existent
  'instructionProcessor' field (InstructionProcessor is not in TestCasesGenerator)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(network): fix 4G relay reservation dispatch (v0.1.33)

- Remove relay_server from client MetaverseBehaviour — clients should
  not relay for others; this eliminates the relay::server 10s idle timer
  that was keeping connections alive just long enough to mask the real bug

- Defer listen_via_relay from ConnectionEstablished to Identify::Received
  — Identify fires after libp2p deduplicates multiple transports (TCP+WS),
  so the reservation request goes to the surviving connection's handler,
  not the one that gets closed by dedup

- Add circuit_registered: HashSet<PeerId> to track which relay peers have
  an active circuit reservation. Guards against duplicate listen_via_relay
  calls and cleared on ConnectionClosed (num_established == 0)

- Increase idle_connection_timeout from 60s to 300s as safety net

- Update connect_to_bootstrap re-registration to filter by circuit_registered

Root cause: libp2p-relay 0.21.1 priv_client.rs uses .first() to select
which connection handler receives a listen_on dispatch. Both TCP and WS
circuit listen_on calls landed on the TCP handler (connected first).
When TCP closed due to dedup, both reservations were lost. WS handler
had reservation=None so connection_keep_alive() returned false.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: align cross-module interfaces for fences, headings, and nav scroll

Three interface mismatches fixed:

1. markdown_fence.rs + highlight.rs: accepted arbitrarily-indented fences
   and headings (trim_start strips all whitespace). CommonMark limits ATX
   heading and code fence indentation to 0-3 spaces. Lines with 4+ spaces
   are indented code blocks, not headings/fences. This caused highlight.rs
   to color code blocks as headings, and format.rs to preserve trailing
   whitespace inside pseudo-fences that pulldown_cmark never recognizes.

2. render.rs heading_y vs nav_outline: heading_y counted ALL Block::Heading
   variants (including empty ones like '# '), but nav_outline::extract_headings
   skips empty headings. This ordinal mismatch caused preview_nav_scroll_y
   to navigate to wrong positions when documents contained empty headings.
   Fixed heading_y to skip empty headings, aligning with nav_outline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Align strategy names with registry keys for correct lastScraped lookup

Strategy constructors used short names ('checking', 'portfolios',
'foreignCurrency') but the registry and strategySync use the full keys
('checking-accounts', 'investment-portfolios', 'foreign-currency').
This caused getScraperOptionsForStrategy(this.name) to miss the
stored lastScraped date and fall back to 6 months on every scrape.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Include choice_map in ask_user responses for deterministic choice values (#27)

* Include choice_map in ask_user responses for deterministic choice values

The LLM agent was mapping 'Wait for another approver' to 'wait_approver'
instead of 'wait_for_approver', causing an unrecognized choice error.
Rather than relying on the LLM to look up SKILL.md, every ask_user
response now includes a choice_map dictionary that maps each display
text to its exact choice value.

commit.message:
Fix: panel scale mismatch and invisible scatter on project load

Panel scale: save sceneUnitsPerMeter per panel during serialize and
use it during restore via _createPanelObject override parameter.
Previously panels were recreated with DEFAULT_SCENE_UNITS_PER_METER
(0.2) instead of the calibrated value, causing wrong sizes.

Scatter: set finalOnly=false when loading saved projects so base
scatter (green dots) renders immediately via 1.5s fallback timer.
Previously finalOnly=true meant dots only appeared after full shading
raycasts completed, which could take very long or hang silently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
